intro: Discover the best supplements to boost your running performance. From caffeine to energy gels, here's an evidence-based selection.

faq.q: What supplements help you run faster?
faq.a: Caffeine, beta-alanine and maltodextrin-based gels are among the most research-backed for improving running performance.

faq.q: When should you take running supplements?
faq.a: Energy gels should be taken during runs (every 45-60 min), caffeine 30-60 minutes before.

intro: Recovery is fundamental for every runner. Here are the products that can help you bounce back faster after training.

faq.q: What should you take after running for recovery?
faq.a: Whey protein within 30 minutes, magnesium to prevent cramps and a foam roller for myofascial release.

faq.q: Does magnesium help muscle recovery?
faq.a: Yes, magnesium contributes to normal muscle function and can reduce cramps and fatigue.

intro: Proper hydration is essential for every runner. Discover the best accessories to stay hydrated during your runs.

faq.q: How much water should you drink while running?
faq.a: On average 400-800ml per hour, depending on temperature, intensity and individual sweat rate.

faq.q: Do you need electrolyte tablets?
faq.a: For runs over 60 minutes or in hot conditions, yes. They replenish sodium and potassium lost through sweat.

intro: Want to lose weight through running? Here are the products that can support your weight loss journey in a healthy, science-backed way.

faq.q: Does running help you lose weight?
faq.a: Yes, running burns approximately 1 kcal/kg/km. Combined with a moderate calorie deficit, it's one of the most effective activities.

faq.q: Do proteins help with weight loss?
faq.a: Proteins increase satiety and preserve muscle mass during a calorie deficit.

intro: The right shoes make all the difference in trail running. Discover how to choose and the recommended models for your off-road adventures.

faq.q: What's the difference between trail and road shoes?
faq.a: Trail shoes have lugged soles for grip on uneven terrain, rock protection and often a more durable upper.

faq.q: How often should you replace trail shoes?
faq.a: On average every 500-800 km, depending on terrain and model.

intro: Preventing injuries is key to running long-term. Here are the best tools and products for recovery and injury prevention.

faq.q: How to prevent running injuries?
faq.a: Stretching, foam rolling, gradual volume progression and shoes suited to your foot strike.

faq.q: Does foam rolling really work?
faq.a: Yes, studies show foam rolling reduces DOMS and improves joint mobility.

intro: Your running shoes are the most important piece of gear. Compare the best performance road running shoes for speed and comfort.

faq.q: Are carbon-plated shoes worth it?
faq.a: For race day and tempo runs, carbon plates can improve running economy by 2-4%. They're best for distances 10K and above.

faq.q: How much drop should a performance shoe have?
faq.a: Most modern performance shoes have 6-10mm drop. Lower drop suits midfoot strikers, higher drop for heel strikers.

intro: Trail runs often take you far from water sources. The right hydration gear is critical for safety and performance on the trails.

faq.q: What's the best hydration system for trail running?
faq.a: Hydration vests with soft flasks are the most popular choice. They distribute weight evenly and allow hands-free drinking.

faq.q: How much water should you carry on a trail run?
faq.a: As a rule of thumb, carry 500ml per hour of running. In hot conditions or long distances, increase to 750ml-1L per hour.

intro: Trail running is especially demanding on muscles and joints. The right recovery supplements can help you bounce back from tough mountain sessions.

faq.q: Why is recovery harder after trail runs?
faq.a: Trail running involves more eccentric muscle contractions from downhill running, causing greater muscle damage and longer recovery times.

faq.q: What supplements help with trail running recovery?
faq.a: Whey protein for muscle repair, tart cherry juice for inflammation, magnesium for cramps and electrolytes for rehydration.

intro: Building endurance requires smart nutrition. Discover the supplements that help you run longer and stronger during marathon training.

faq.q: What supplements improve running endurance?
faq.a: Beetroot juice (nitrates), caffeine, sodium bicarbonate and proper carbohydrate loading are the most evidence-based endurance boosters.

faq.q: Should long-distance runners take iron supplements?
faq.a: Only if blood tests show low ferritin levels. Runners are prone to iron deficiency due to foot-strike hemolysis and sweat losses.

intro: Trail running puts unique stress on ankles, knees and stabilizer muscles. Here are the best tools to prevent trail-specific injuries.

faq.q: What are the most common trail running injuries?
faq.a: Ankle sprains, IT band syndrome, patellofemoral pain and plantar fasciitis are the most common. Strength training and proper shoes help prevent them.

faq.q: How to strengthen ankles for trail running?
faq.a: Single-leg balance exercises, resistance band work and proprioception training on unstable surfaces are highly effective.

intro: From GPS watches to heart rate monitors, the right accessories can transform your training data and help you reach new PRs.

faq.q: Do I need a running watch?
faq.a: A GPS watch helps track pace, distance and heart rate zones. It's valuable for structured training but not essential for casual runners.

faq.q: What running accessories improve performance?
faq.a: GPS watches for pacing, heart rate straps for zone training, and compression socks for reduced fatigue on long runs.
